rulings.Case Title: TERREE ROUSH et al VS. THE COCA-COLA COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 3, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT THE COCA-COLA COMPANY AND DELPHI PRODUCTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The moving parties have shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's discovery responses. The motion of Delphi and Coca-Cola is denied because the declaration of Brooks creates a triable issue of fact as to whether the slide was defectively designed in such a manner as to create an extreme risk of danger of the type that caused plaintiff's injury. Branco v. Kearny Moto Park, Inc. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 184. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: TERREE ROUSH et al VS. THE COCA-COLA COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 3, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT CHINA BASIN BALLPARK COMPANY, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The motion of China Basin as to the negligence claim is denied. The Brooks' declaration creates a triable issue of fact within the meaning of the decision in Branco v. Kearny Moto Park, Inc. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 184. The motion is granted as to the products liability claim because plaintiff has failed to raise a triable issue of fact that this defendant was a "link in the chain". San Diego Hospital Assn. v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 8, 15. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN J JOHNSON et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 3, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The motion is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof because plaintiff's discovery responses fail to identify decedent's exposure to a product manufactured or distributed by defendant that contained asbestos. Defendant could not be expected to have deposed W.R. Johnson before the filing of this motion given the September trial date and the fact that Mr. Johnson was disclosed only three days before defendant filed its motion. W.R. Johnson's declaration is admissible. However, plaintiff has not shown that the deposition testimony of Messers. Allen and LeCour are admissible. The testimony is hearsay and no showing has been made that either witness is unavailable as required by Evidence Code § 1292(a)(1). Absent that testimony, plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of fact that plaintiff had contact with a product manufactured or distributed by Abex that contained asbestos. =(302/PJM/KD)
